evaluation: Top performers qualify for Round 2.
format: Written test on logical reasoning, analytical skills, basic problem-solving.
name: Aptitude Test

evaluation: Clarity and fluency, Confidence and stage presence, Relevance and strength of content.
format: Spontaneous topic given on the spot; must deliver a structured speech without prior preparation.
name: Effective Public Speaking

evaluation: Creativity and spontaneity, Presence of mind, Ability to handle stress effectively.
format: Participants dress as a chosen character; judges ask situational and character-based questions.
name: Stress Interview

evaluation: Voice quality, pitch, rhythm and stage confidence.
format: Theme: Free Choice. Any song in any language. Karaoke allowed.
name: Open Singing Round

evaluation: Expression, creativity and overall performance style.
format: Theme: Emotion & Style. Song must express emotion; facial expressions and gestures allowed.
name: Expression Round

evaluation: Exact output match including formatting, spacing and structure.
format: Analyse the provided output and implement the logic strictly using C.
name: Reverse Coding – C Programming

evaluation: Exact output match including formatting, spacing and structure.
format: Analyse the provided output and implement the logic strictly using Python.
name: Reverse Coding – Python Programming

evaluation: Accuracy in identifying valid clues and adherence to system usage guidelines.
format: Teams analyse the provided system to locate hidden digital clues without altering system settings.
name: System Exploration

evaluation: Team that successfully completes all levels in the shortest time wins.
format: Teams explore authorized physical locations and solve advanced challenges to progress through levels.
name: Physical & Advanced Clue Hunt

evaluation: Creativity, clarity of concept, and feasibility of execution.
format: Participants receive the theme/product and prepare a storyboard or conceptual plan.
name: Concept & Planning

evaluation: Originality, storytelling, technical quality and adherence to theme.
format: Teams shoot and edit the promotional video. Duration: strictly 1–3 minutes.
name: Video Creation

evaluation: Overall impact, clarity of presentation and quality of final output.
format: Final videos screened before judges. Each team may give a brief 1-minute explanation.
name: Presentation & Screening

evaluation: Systematic observation, correct identification of components and proper deconstruction methodology.
format: Observe the given product; physically dismantle hardware or use approved software tools.
name: Observation & Deconstruction

evaluation: Accuracy in identifying the underlying logic, clarity of explanation and technical correctness.
format: Analyse the identified components to determine the logic and functioning of the system.
name: Analysis & Logic Extraction

evaluation: Accuracy of spelling and completeness of recalled content.
format: Images and names will be displayed on the screen for a limited duration.
name: Visual Display Round

evaluation: Marks awarded based on correctness and proper order of responses.
format: Participants must recall and write answers in the correct sequence as displayed.
name: Sequence-Based Round

evaluation: Teams ranked by calculated score.
format: Rockets launched for maximum horizontal distance. Score = Distance × Time-of-Flight × 0.02
name: Distance Challenge

evaluation: Points awarded based on landing zone.
format: Aim at target 65m away. Inner (10m): 20pts, Middle (15m): 15pts, Outer (20m): 10pts.
name: Target Accuracy

evaluation: Performance based on speed and successful completion.
format: A soft drink chugging task or a basic speed eating challenge.
name: Quick Bite Battle

evaluation: Based on total quantity consumed within the official time limit.
format: A time-based eating round — consume as much food as possible within the allotted time.
name: Maximum Munch Mode

evaluation: Correct identification of character and series.
format: Blurred or cropped anime character images displayed. Identify the character name and anime series.
name: Character Reveal

evaluation: Correct anime title identification.
format: A scene or symbol is shown. Identify the anime title.
name: Guess the Anime

evaluation: Correct character/anime identification.
format: A famous anime dialogue is displayed. Guess the character or the anime.
name: Dialogue Challenge

evaluation: Speed and accuracy; double points apply.
format: 5–10 quick questions with a shorter time limit. Double points in this round.
name: Rapid Fire (Final Round)
